Welcome back to Exercise 3. This is the mic stand moment and this was actually inspired by Todd Herman's 90-day year event a few years ago. So I worked with him on that launch and a few months later I had the opportunity to go to the event which was a bonus for all the people who had invested in the program. So I'm sitting in the back of the room on day one just in full out observation mode and there's about I think something like 300 people in attendance and every now and then throughout the morning Todd would open up the microphones for people to share their experience in the 90-day year. And what I found amazing here was the level of emotionality that people would have when they would rush up to that stand and just gush about their experience and how it impacted them and these are the kinds of reviews and testimonials and case studies I was never really getting through traditional means. So there's something about the energy of that room about just being surrounded by other people who had shared that experience that really allowed them to open up and feel like that was the right forum and place to go deep and reveal the intimate details of their life and their experience that they may not otherwise do. So it is tough to do at scale unless you are running events and seminars and things of that sort in which case I would highly recommend you rewatch those opportunities when people do actually take the mic stand. But otherwise if you're running this with a client try to paint that visualization with them have them in a scenario where they are giving the live event version of their program filled with people who had invested in it and you have someone just that perfect client rush up to the stand and say something that would move you as a creator that would make you realize as the creator of this program that you absolutely nailed it with your offer and your delivery and your product essentially just fulfilled all the reasons it is on this planet to exist. So yeah and that visualization just try to recap what made that person join. Be very clear about what their starting point was that's going to reveal a lot with the moment of highest tension. What previous challenges were they having in relation to that outcome so that might be the sacred cows and then of course what does their life look like now. Those are going to be your benefits the moment of highest pleasure redemption all that good stuff and why are they so grateful and happy that they took you up on your offer. What would they be saying that would make you once again as a creator feel completely fulfilled like you achieved the highest possible outcome with your product or offer. In essence what would make you tear up in hearing their experience using your creation. So that's the mic stand moment I encourage you to do that with your client or as a solo exercise just really tuning in to tuning into that vision visualization as deeply as you can and I will see you in exercise four.
